mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth is negative, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 250.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, negative real interest rates compress equity valuations and force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit expectations, which mechanically supports equity prices and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.0.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate signals a broad-based economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in walk-forward tests.
